theorem RCLike.norm_I {K : Type u_1} [RCLike K] : ‖(RCLike.I : K)‖ = if RCLike.I ≠ (0 : K) then 1 else 0 := by
  split_ifs with h
  · apply RCLike.norm_I_of_ne_zero h
  · push_neg at h
    simpa

  with respect to `μ` and `p'`-Lipschitz with respect to `ν` (in the sense of `HasLipschitz`)
  and `T` is weakly continuous from `L^p(μ)` to `L^{p'}(ν)`.
  Then `T` is of Lorentz type `L^{p,p'}(μ,ν)`.

  This is a generalization of the classical result that maps of the form
  `f ↦ ∫_{Y} k(x,y) f(y) dν(y)` from `L^p(ν)` to `L^{p'}(μ)` are of Lorentz type
  if `k` satisfies the appropriate Hölder conditions.
-/

theorem HasLorentzType.hasLorentzType''''' {T : (α → ℝ) → (α' → ℝ) → (ℝ≥0)}
    {p p' : ℝ≥0∞}
    {μ : Measure α} {ν : Measure α'}
    {c : ℝ≥0} (c_pos : 0 < c)
    (hT : HasLipschitz T p μ c) (hT' : HasLipschitz T p' ν c)
    (hT_ww : HasWeaklyContinuous T p μ ν c) :
    HasLorentzType T p 1 p' ∞ μ ν c := by
  apply HasLorentzType.ofHasLipschitzAndWeaklyContinuous hT hT' hT_ww
